title: leucocroton subpeltatus
intro: Leucocroton subpeltatus, also known as the yellow leucocroton, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and broad, ovate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Leucocroton subpeltatus include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, treat wounds and skin conditions, and act as an antiseptic to prevent infections. The plant is typically prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and may also be applied topically as a paste or salve to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
